title: ContextContinuationV2
description: I-010D ContextContinuationV2@1 — host-only bounded context expansion, encoding the design's ContextFetch/ContextPage shapes verbatim (FR-014, c834e8c): the directional anchor-bearing fetch request (opaque handle, direction, anchor, opaque cursor, positive per-fetch budgets) and the identity-bearing fetch page (room and continuity-scope identity, direction, anchor, actor map, authoritative-order events, returned coverage, opaque next cursor). The continuation capability itself (`handle_id`, exact `bound_to`, before/after/around fetch capabilities, per-fetch caps, optional expiry) is defined on I-010A/`attention-request.schema.json#/$defs/continuation`; this schema covers only the fetch request/page pair the capability authorizes. Handles, cursors, and fetch credentials are host-only and forbidden from the classifier projection. Fetch-time binding/expiry validation, cross-binding cursor reuse, and the continuation-page/originating-request duplicate-ID merge-identity rule are runtime-adapter-only (FR-012).

anchor_event_id.$comment: Defaults to the trigger for before/after and is required for around (enforced below).

cursor.$comment: Opaque; absent starts from the binding's beginning.

events.$comment: Authoritative order; exact merge identity against the originating request is runtime-adapter-only.

next_cursor.$comment: Opaque; absent means the binding is exhausted.
